title: Worcester man receives life sentence for home invasion
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Concepcion Rodriguez, 39, sentenced to life for a 2022 Fitchburg home invasion, plus additional time for eight other charges.
Rodriguez was convicted of armed assault, firearm possession, and larceny; one gun possession charge was dismissed.
Defense plans to appeal, claiming Rodriguez was not properly warned of habitual criminal penalties during sentencing.

title: Derrick White talks beer, being a Bostonian, and a promising Celtics playoff run
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Derrick White, Julian Edelman, Zdeno Chara, and Garrett Crochet featured in Sam Adams' new beer line, celebrating at an event in Boston.
White, feeling like a true Bostonian, has embraced local culture while playing a key role in the Celtics' success.
Celtics ranked second in Eastern Conference with 53-25 record as Jayson Tatum returns, boosting playoff aspirations.

title: Mike Krzyzewski threw a subtle jab at Bill Belichick in response to new UNC hire
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: UNC hired former Denver Nuggets coach Michael Malone as the new men's basketball coach, replacing Hubert Davis.
Mike Krzyzewski commented on UNC's hire, noting the challenges faced by Bill Belichick in his first season coaching football.
Belichick and Malone, both with professional success, face college head coaching roles without prior college head coaching experience.

title: Person stabs dog in Milton during alleged attack
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A person stabbed a dog after it allegedly attacked them in Milton, according to State Police.
The incident occurred Monday morning on Blue Hill River Road, a location near a local park.
The dog was taken to a local vet, and Milton Animal Control is investigating the incident.

title: Alex Cora reshuffles Red Sox lineup, drops Trevor Story out of No. 2 spot
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Trevor Story moved to No. 5 spot in Red Sox lineup after a .119 batting average start.
Masataka Yoshida will bat second, with Roman Anthony leading off against the Brewers.
Red Sox aim to improve 3.3 runs per game average, ranking low in walks and high in strikeouts.

title: Cleveland radio host says Cavaliers are ‘scared’ of facing the Celtics in the playoffs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cleveland Cavaliers seek to secure the No. 4 seed to potentially avoid facing Boston Celtics in the playoffs.
Celtics have dominated recent matchups against Cavaliers, sweeping the regular-season series and defeating them in 2024 playoffs.
Cavs are dealing with injuries and lineup uncertainties, possibly influencing their playoff seeding strategy as regular season ends.

title: Where you live in Mass. can shape your electric bill — is yours getting out of control?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts residents have the highest electricity costs in the continental U.S., varying greatly by service provider.
42 municipal utilities serve 52 Massachusetts communities, often offering electricity at half the cost of investor-owned utilities.
Investor-owned utilities like Eversource earned nearly 16 cents profit per dollar, above the national average of 13 cents.

title: Northeastern students rally over lack of communication after recent violence
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Northeastern students protested inadequate communication from the university following violent incidents near campus.
A recent fatal police shooting near campus has increased reliance on police updates over university alerts.
Northeastern has increased patrols and promises to keep the community informed, citing off-campus incident concerns.

title: BU president pauses signage policy after Pride flag controversy
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston University pauses signage policy after protests over Pride flag removals from three campus locations.
Petition and protests by faculty, students, and alumni prompted the temporary pause in signage removal policy.
President Melissa Gilliam emphasizes creating dialogue and supporting the LGBTQ+ community amid the policy review.

title: Red Sox complete Sonny Gray trade, send pitching prospect to Cardinals
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Red Sox sent pitching prospect Patrick Galle to the Cardinals to complete the Sonny Gray trade.
Patrick Galle, a right-handed pitcher, was selected by Boston in the 17th round of the 2025 draft.
Sonny Gray allowed four runs in his Red Sox debut in a 6-5 loss against the Reds.
